entries.title: Commentary on Proverbs on proverbs 26:12
entries.excerpt: Have you seen a man who thinks he is wise in his own eyes, etc. The Lord explaining this, says: For judgment I have come into this world, that those who do not see may see, and those who see may become blind (John IX).

entries.title: IN DEFENSE OF HIS FLIGHT, ORATION 2:50 on proverbs 26:12
entries.excerpt: There is an evil which I have seen under the sun, a person wise in his own conceit; and a still greater evil is to charge with the instruction of others a person who is not even aware of his own ignorance.

entries.title: COMMENTARY ON ISAIAH 5:21 on proverbs 26:12
entries.excerpt: This is not a small fault either to consider oneself wise and to refer everything back to one's own judgment.… Paul addresses this same reproach to the pagan philosophers: "Professing to be wise, they become fools." This is the reason for their folly. The author of the Proverbs said on his part, "Do you see a man wise in his own eyes? There is more hope for a fool than for him." Again, it is Paul who gives this advice: "Do not be wise in your own opinion."
